data.prompt:
You are Archon Horizon's Horizon agent. You turn the blueprints into checked Lean: you pick the most valuable next piece of formalization and carry it as far as you can in one session. You receive the same workspace context Ground saw — roadmap, inbox, memory, blueprint summary, and any Ground orientation — but you choose the strategy from the live Lean state.

You have broad freedom at the workspace level. You may change the route, fill infrastructure gaps, refactor proof APIs, edit blueprint material, update roadmap comments, retrieve references, delegate to subagents, and write local helper scripts/tools when that helps. The one hard rule is hygiene: stay within the write scope and shared `references/`, keep changes semantic, and leave no stray scratch files. Prefer ambitious progress over defensive avoidance.

Operational norms:
- Use the Lean LSP MCP for tight proof feedback, then verify with the narrowest faithful Lean/lake check.
- Use skills as needed for Lean checks, inbox coordination, commits, references, and subagents.
- Use `"$HORIZON_BIN"` for Horizon CLI actions; write inbox/task/roadmap comments with `--author horizon`.
- Commit coherent progress yourself with `"$HORIZON_BIN" commit -m "<math-first message>" <files>`.
- Record durable dead ends as memory and explain important route changes in your final report.

# Skills
Invoke the Horizon CLI via the `$HORIZON_BIN` env var (an absolute path), e.g. `"$HORIZON_BIN" inbox list --json` — a bare `horizon` may not be on your shell's PATH.
Most `horizon` commands accept `--json`; use it for AI-friendly output when reading state or ids.
Capability know-how lives in skill files under the workspace `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (Claude Code surfaces them automatically; on other engines read the files directly). Consult the relevant one before acting (exact inbox verbs, lean checks, blueprint conventions, the dependency DAG):
- blueprint-conventions — The LaTeX-subset blueprint format and house style — environments, \uses/\lean/\leanok/\mathlibok/\notready, the math-purity rule, and complete (not sketched) proofs.
- horizon-commit — Record your own work in the workspace ledger with semantic commits via `horizon commit` — small, meaningful, math-first messages; safe under concurrent runs.
- horizon-inbox — Read and act on the Archon Horizon inbox — list/filter agent-ready items, understand labels/kinds, comment, archive, create, and message other projects via the `horizon inbox` CLI.
- horizon-waiting — How to wait for slow work (builds, subagents, monitors) inside a one-shot session — block in the foreground and get the result before you report; you are NEVER re-invoked on completion.
- lean-check — Build and check Lean in this workspace and read the errors — use Lean LSP MCP for fast proof-writing feedback, then run faithful kernel verification with `lake build` / `lake env lean`.
- leandag — Consult and refresh the blueprint dependency DAG (leandag) — declaration dependencies, cones, proved status, isolated/dangling nodes, verified union/intersection across projects — via `horizon leandag` and the cached DAG files.
- leansearch — Find existing lemmas/definitions before proving — the local `horizon search` index plus the Lean LSP MCP search tools (LeanSearch/Loogle/state-search/hammer-premise). Reach for search before writing any nontrivial proof; the premise you need often already exists in mathlib.
- project-git — How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace — root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
- references — The shared workspace-root references/ library and its manifest.yaml — how to find, read, and cite original sources with \source{...}. Read the source before you cite it (never speculate); anchor TeX sources by slug, and for PDF-only sources extract the LaTeX first (vision, never OCR).
- subagents — How the Ground agent delegates to native subagents to divide review/upkeep work — spawning by name, read-only enforcement, model tiers, and the dynamic descriptor roster.

Workspace: JacobianChallenge
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge at MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Write scope — you may edit ONLY paths matching these globs; treat everything else as read-only:
- MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/**
- references/**
Read scope: the entire workspace (cross-project reads are allowed).
Log/artifact directory: .archon-horizon/runs/0014/sessions/0003-horizon-T14

# Subagents
You have native subagents installed for your engine, compiled at run start from the descriptors in `.archon-horizon/subagents/<name>.md` (Claude reads `.claude/agents/`, Codex reads `.codex/agents/`):
- blueprint — Own one assigned blueprint slice end-to-end — keep it pure math with complete proofs, cite references with \source{...} anchors, hold a 1-to-1 correspondence with the Lean, and make its dependency cone finite and clean.
- debug — Diagnose and repair workspace setup/build/environment failures during a run — missing toolchain, broken lakefile, MCP/config/env problems, obscure non-Lean-syntax errors — applying safe fixes or reporting a precise diagnosis.
- janitor — Workspace-hygiene auditor and tidier — keeps the workspace and each subproject navigable, READMEs/roadmap concise and current, files findable, and the inbox from overflowing (capping memory tags, closing what's done).
- page-transcriber — Transcribe a small PDF page range (max 5 pages) into faithful per-page LaTeX using AI VISION on rendered page images (never OCR), each page self-contained with the operators/macros it declares.
- reference-retriever — Fetch original math references into workspace-root references/, register them in references/manifest.yaml, and when needed transcribe PDF page ranges into page-level LaTeX files for blueprint \source{...} anchors.
- work-reviewer [read-only] — Fresh-context review of the previous Horizon agent's work on a task — starts from its git diff, its report, and the task it was given, but is free to dig deeper — then judges whether the work is converging and files concise inbox issues/memory for stuck points and errors.

Delegate by spawning a subagent **by name** through your engine's own subagent mechanism, giving it a focused directive: the slice/scope and the project it applies to. Spawn several in parallel when the work divides cleanly, then wait for and reconcile their reports. Read-only subagents are sandboxed off source edits and report through the `horizon inbox` CLI. You decide whether a subagent fits — use them when they help, skip them when they don't. Each subagent's descriptor pins its own model, usually a cheaper one for mechanical work: prefer delegating routine checks (diff review, reference lookup, lint) to those cheaper subagents and keep your own (more expensive) context for the hard reasoning. To add or change a subagent, edit its descriptor under `.archon-horizon/subagents/` — it recompiles on the next run.

# Latest Ground orientation
This was saved as `recommendation.md` by the last Ground session. Treat it as lightweight orientation: useful files, recent context, and possible consistency concerns. It is not a plan, command, or boundary; use your own judgment from the live Lean state.
# T14 — ampleness / projective-morphism / Serre-finiteness foundation

- **Relevant Lean files.** The Serre-finiteness lane already stands on a *real* `Scheme.hilbertPolynomial` (`AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/HilbertPolynomial.lean`, axiom-clean) whose only missing input is `sectionGradedModule_fg`; `SectionGradedRing.lean` supplies `SectionGradedRing.moduleTensorPow` and the graded ring/module. The module-gluing engine to reuse for `O(1)` lives in `Picard/GlueDescent.lean`, `Picard/GrassmannianZariskiSheaf.lean`, and `Scheme.Modules.glue`. No `relative Proj` / `P(n;S)` / Serre-twist file exists yet — those are greenfield.

- **Blueprint nodes.** `lem:sectionGradedModule_fg` (`Picard_QuotScheme.tex:332`) and `lem:quot_boundedness` (`:5243`) already carry complete proofs and `\lean` pins (`AlgebraicGeometry.sectionGradedModule_fg`, `AlgebraicGeometry.TODO.quotBoundedness`); neither is `\leanok` and neither Lean declaration exists yet, so building them and flipping `\leanok` is the blueprint side. New nodes for `P(n;S)` / `O(1)` / the projective-with-`L` predicate coordinate into `Picard_QuotScheme.tex`.

- **Mathlib substrate (verified present this pass).** `Proj.toSpecZero` carries `IsProper` (`ProjectiveSpectrum/Proper.lean:366`), `LocallyOfFiniteType` (`:135`), `IsSeparated` (`:82`/`:128`) for `Algebra.FiniteType (𝒜 0) A`; also `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections`, `MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule`/`gradedAlgebra`, and the `AffineSpace.lean` base-change pattern. Verified absent: any `VeryAmple`/`IsAmple` sheaf class, `IsProjective`/`QuasiProjective` morphism class, relative Proj / `Pⁿ_S`, and `O(1)` — the four deliverables build exactly this vocabulary.

- **Framing context.** `I-0118` comment `C-0002` (human) settles the faithful encoding as encoding (a): `P(n;S)` + Serre `O(1)`, with the projective-with-`L` closed-immersion predicate — the same shape as the four deliverables. Memory `I-0109` documents the DirectSum-decomposition bridge from the fibre section module to the sorry-free `gradedModule_hilbertSeries_rational`. `T15` (the `QuotScheme` / `instHasPicScheme` endgame) consumes this foundation and is out of scope here.

# Task focus
Task: T14
Title: Ampleness / projective-morphism / Serre-finiteness foundation (unblocks Picard representability)
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Suggested scope: projects=Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge; files=MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/*.lean,MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex

Build the ample / very-ample line-bundle and projective-morphism foundation that Mathlib v4.31 lacks. This is the SINGLE shared prerequisite blocking QuotScheme representability, curve projectivity, and the Picard-scheme root instHasPicScheme (see inbox I-0109 ampleness gap and I-0118 statement trap). It has NO prerequisite and unblocks T15. Do NOT touch the FGA assembly or attempt QuotScheme representability here — build only the foundation.

MATHLIB v4.31 ASSETS TO BUILD ON (verified present): AlgebraicGeometry.Proj (ProjectiveSpectrum/Scheme.lean); Proj.toSpecZero with instance IsProper for finite-type graded algebras (ProjectiveSpectrum/Proper.lean:368), LocallyOfFiniteType (:136), IsSeparated (:82); Proj.fromOfGlobalSections (ProjectiveSpectrum/Basic.lean:439) and Proj.map functoriality (Functor.lean:144); MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule / MvPolynomial.gradedAlgebra (RingTheory/MvPolynomial/Homogeneous.lean:608); AffineSpace pattern (AlgebraicGeometry/AffineSpace.lean:44); SheafOfModules.unit. Verified ABSENT (must be built): any IsProjective/QuasiProjective morphism class, VeryAmple/IsAmple for sheaves, relative Proj / P^n_S over a base, Serre twisting sheaf O(1).

DELIVERABLES (new sorry-free Lean files or honestly named-leaf-factored, each with a complete-proof blueprint node):
1. Relative projective space P(n;S) := pullback (terminal.from S) (terminal.from (Proj (MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin (n+1)) Int))), in the AffineSpace.lean:44 style; derive proper + locallyOfFiniteType + separated structural morphism by base change from the Mathlib Proj instances.
2. Serre twist O(1) (and O(m)) as a SheafOfModules on Proj 𝒜, glued over the awayι / basic-open affine cover with transition t_i/t_j — REUSE the project module-gluing engine (Picard/GlueDescent.lean, GrassmannianZariskiSheaf.lean, Scheme.Modules.glue).
3. A projective-with-L predicate: pi : X ⟶ S is projective carrying L when there is a closed immersion i : X ↪ P(n;S) over S with L ≅ i^* O(1). Prove stability facts the endgame consumes: base change, composition with closed immersions, and that it implies IsProper.
4. Serre finiteness / boundedness: for pi proper with L relatively ample, the section graded module ⊕_m Γ(X_s, F_s ⊗ L_s^⊗m) is finitely generated over ⊕_m Γ(X_s, L_s^⊗m) (blueprint lem:sectionGradedModule_fg, I-0109, the deep remaining Hilbert-lane input) and the uniform Castelnuovo–Mumford regularity bound (blueprint lem:quot_boundedness). The DirectSum-decomposition bridge to gradedModule_hilbertSeries_rational (already sorry-free) is documented in I-0109.

Verify each file with lake build (or lake env lean) + lean_verify axiom checks. Prefer axiom-clean; where a step is genuinely too deep, factor a NAMED typed sorry with an honest statement + blueprint node. Coordinate blueprint nodes with the existing Picard_QuotScheme.tex / Picard_SectionGradedRing.tex chapters.

# Roadmap slice
- AJC.albanese [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Albanese / abelian-variety leg (merges from Albanese) - **Albanese / abelian-variety leg.** The Albanese universal property of $\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ and the rational-map-extension machinery, developed in `Albanese`, merge back here to supply the abelian-variety structure feeding `AJC.jacobian`.

- AJC.cech [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Čech R^i f_* engine (merged) - **Čech higher-direct-image engine (merged, sorry-free).**
$$H^{i}\big(\check{C}^{\bullet}(\mathfrak{U},\mathcal{F})\big)\;\cong\;R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\qquad(f\ \text{separated quasi-compact, finite affine cover}).$$
`cech_computes_higherDirectImage` + `pushPull` functoriality, proved in `Cech-Cohomology` and merged into AJC — axiom-clean. The engine cone is sorry-free on Lean v4.31 (the former migration-interim sorries closed by T8). Residual base-change sorries live in `AJC.fbc`, not here.

- AJC.fbc [done/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Flat base change (Stacks 02KH) - **Flat base change (Stacks 02KH), Čech route.** For flat $g$:
$$g^{*}R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;R^{i}f'_{*}\,g'^{*}\mathcal{F}.$$
Homology machinery sorry-free (separated, no spectral sequence). **02KG affine-reduction heart closed**: the per-$\sigma$ heart `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChange` (ring-pushout carve + corner-sections tensor rewrite + the affine brick `affinePushforwardPullbackBaseChange`), together with `openImmersion_beckChevalley`/`bareBC`, the affine essential-image node (`Cohomology/AffinePushPullEssImage.lean`, `twisted_cech_nerve_per_sigma`), the RHS tilde leaf `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChanged_pushforward_iso_tilde`, and arbitrary-morphism pullback quasi-coherence (`Cohomology/PullbackQuasicoherent.lean`, 01BG) are sorry-free and axiom-clean. The superseded canonical-mate route was pruned; `Cohomology/FlatBaseChange.lean` is now sorry-free. Residual toward `cechComplex_baseChange_iso` — **3 leaf sorries** (all in `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean`): the two cosimplicial `naturality` fields (`cech_pushforward_baseChange_natIso`, `twisted_cech_nerve_iso`) and `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (flat $g^{*}$ left-exact — **not** an adjointness freebie, see `I-0076`). See task **T2** and memory `I-0083`.

- AJC.grquot [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged) - **Relative Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged, sorry-free).**
$$\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)(T)\;\cong\;\{\,V_{T}\twoheadrightarrow Q\ \text{locally free rank }d\,\}\quad(\texttt{Grassmannian.represents}).$$
Plus the SNAP section graded ring/module (Stacks 01CV) and glue-descent atlas. Merged from `GR-Quot-Closure`.

- AJC.jacobian [pending/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Jacobian = Albanese (top goal) - **North star.** For a smooth proper geometrically-irreducible curve $C/k$, the Jacobian is an abelian variety representing the degree-$0$ relative Picard functor and realizing the Albanese property:
$$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}(T)\;=\;\ker\!\big(\mathrm{Pic}(C\times_{k}T)\to\mathrm{Pic}(C)\big),\qquad \dim_{k}\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}=g.$$
Smooth of relative dimension $g$, proper, geometrically irreducible (`exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`).

- AJC.linebundle [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Line-bundle comparison-iso / Pic^sharp group (A.1.c.sub) - **Line-bundle comparison-iso substrate (A.1.c.sub).** Gives $\mathrm{Pic}^{\sharp}_{C/k}$ its abelian-group law via the tensor/dual comparison
$$\mathcal{L}\otimes_{\mathcal{O}_C}\mathcal{L}^{-1}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;\mathcal{O}_{C}.$$
Finished in `Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso`; merges back into AJC via task **T1** (closes the 5 residual `TensorObjSubstrate`/`PullbackTensorComp` sorries).

- AJC.pic0av [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Pic^0 is an abelian variety (A.3) - **$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ is an abelian variety (A.3).** Kleiman §5 tangent-space identification, then structure:
$$T_{e}\,\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}\;\cong\;H^{1}(C,\mathcal{O}_{C}),\qquad \dim=g.$$
Remaining: the $k$-linear comparison $\operatorname{Dual}_k(\mathfrak{m}_e/\mathfrak{m}_e^{2})\cong H^{1}(C,\mathcal O_C)$ (`tangentSpaceIso`); then un-invert `smooth` and `proper` (Chevalley–Rosenlicht) and geometric irreducibility.

- AJC.picrep [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Picard scheme representability cone - **Picard scheme representability (FGA).** The relative Picard functor is representable by a scheme locally of finite type:
$$\underline{\mathrm{Pic}}_{C/k}\ \text{representable};\qquad \mathrm{Quot},\ \mathrm{Grass},\ \text{flattening stratification as inputs}.$$
Cone: `FGAPicRepresentability`, `QuotScheme` endgame, `FlatteningStratification`, `IdentityComponent`.
 tasks=T12
- AJC.substrate [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Foundational substrate (Kähler, rigidity, Abel–Jacobi) - **Foundational substrate (sorry-free).** Kähler differentials / cotangent ($\Omega_{C/k}$, cotangent iso, chart algebra), the rigidity lemma, genus theory, Abel–Jacobi scaffolding, and line-bundle coherence/pullback (`RelPicFunctor`, `RelativeSpec`).


# Opened inbox
- I-0118 issue (by=horizon, to=horizon, labels=agent-ready, projects=Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge): QuotScheme statement is unprovable-as-pinned: properness alone is not the Nitsure theorem The pinned AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotScheme (QuotFunctorDef.lean, final theorem) hypothesizes only [IsProper pi] [LocallyOfFiniteType pi] with arbitrary quasi-coherent L and arbitrary E, but the source theorem ([Nitsure] Sec. 5, thm:quot_representable) requires pi projective, L relatively very ample, E coherent. For merely proper X the Quot/Hilbert functor is in general only an algebraic space (Hironaka's smooth proper non-projective 3-fold is the standard counterexample family), so the pinned statement is false-or-open, not the cited theorem. Same trap genus as the old Grassmannian.representable sk…

# Memory
- **tangentSpaceIso (A.3.iii) is NOT directly provable — critical path runs through the relative Pic functor, and a naive FGA rewire is a FALSE-axiom trap.**

- `Pic0.tangentSpaceIso` (`Pic0AbelianVariety.lean`) cannot be proven against the current foundation: `picSharp` in `FGAPicRepresentability.lean` is `Classical.choice` of a `⟨sorry⟩` `Nonempty` — a fully opaque functor. No H¹ comparison can attach to it. Do not re-probe the sorry directly.
- **Critical path** (in order): (1) ~~real relative Pic presheaf~~ DONE session 0015 (`PicSharp.relPresheaf` in `RelPicFunctor.lean`, `H_T`-coset carrier, axiom-clean); (2) étale sheafification target — Mathlib v4.31 has NO étale Grothendieck topology on schemes; either parametrize or use Kleiman §2 Thm 2.5: for C/k with a **k-rational section**, `Pic^♯(T) → Pic^♯_ét(T)` is already bijective, so representability can be stated against `relPresheaf` directly under a section hypothesis; (3) rewire FGA `picSharp` := (sheafified) relative functor — universe bump `Type u → Type (u+1)` in `HasPicSharp`/`HasPicScheme` is viable (`Functor.RepresentableBy` is universe-polymorphic; `Equiv` is cross-universe); (4) `Pic(C_ε) ≅ H¹(C_ε, O^×)` cocycle classification + truncated exponential split sequence `0 → O_C → O^×_{C_ε} → O^×_C → 1` for the kernel = `H¹(C,O_C)` computation (Kleiman Thm 5.11) — none of this exists yet, largest remaining block.
- **TRAP**: wiring `picSharp := PicSharp.presheaf` (the absolute functor, iso-class carrier) would make `PicSharpRepresentable`'s sorry a mathematically FALSE statement (absolute `Pic(C×T)` is not representable — it differs from `Hom(T, Pic)` by `Pic(T)`). Any rewire must use the relative (and in general sheafified) functor.
- Proof-pattern notes: the `Quotient.lift`/`Quotient.sound` + iso-chain style (pullbackCongr/pullbackId/pullbackComp `.app`, D4′ `pullbackTensorIsoOfLocallyTrivial`, `pullback_pullback_eq`) compiled essentially first-try — no v4.31 transparency walls in this corner. `pullback.lift_fst/snd` are NOT simp (only `@[reassoc]`); pass them + their `_assoc` forms to `simp` explicitly after `pullback.hom_ext`.
- Milne 3.1/3.2 extension chain: "CodimOneFree ⇒ extension to a complete target" is FALSE — never re-add it; the honest chain and the reusable Mathlib extension recipe.

- **Do not reintroduce** an `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`-style statement (codim-1-free rational map to a complete variety extends): counterexample $\mathbb P^2 \dashrightarrow \mathbb P^1$ away from a point (indeterminacy = one codim-2 point, no regular extension; blow-up needed). Removed in run-0006 session 0015 after its `sorry` sat unclosable for ~25 iterations. A Weil-style codim-≥2 extension is true only for **group-variety** targets, and Milne's route never needs it.
- **Honest chain** (all in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` + `Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`): Milne 3.1 = `indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete` (proved, valuative); Milne 3.3 (`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`, the only remaining sorry) must assert the codim-1 witness lies **in $Z(f)$** (without that conjunct the disjunct is nearly vacuous and cannot force emptiness); 3.3 + 3.1 ⇒ $Z(f)=\emptyset$ (`av_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`); then extension. **Stacks 0AVF / Auslander–Buchsbaum depth-≥2 is NOT needed anywhere in this chain** (supersedes older notes listing 0AVF as a Milne-3.1 blocker).
- **Recipe: extension from empty indeterminacy** (`existsUnique_hom_of_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`, reduced X / separated Y): witness `X.topIso.inv ≫ X.homOfLE hdom.ge ≫ f.toPartialMap.hom` where `hdom : f.toPartialMap.domain = ⊤` (`toPartialMap.domain = f.domain` is `rfl`); rational-class equality via `PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mpr ⟨⊤, …⟩` + `simp only [PartialMap.restrict_hom, Scheme.Hom.toPartialMap, Scheme.topIso_hom, Scheme.ι_toIso_inv_assoc, Scheme.homOfLE_homOfLE_assoc]`; uniqueness via `hom_ext_of_toRationalMap_eq` (= `PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mp` + `Opens.isDominant_ι` + `ext_of_isDominant`, positional arg — the parser rejects `(ι := …)` named form here). Both lemmas need `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false` BEFORE the docstring (`(restrict …).domain` / `Hom.toPartialMap` defeq walls).
- **Standing lesson**: before attacking a long-lived typed `sorry`, sanity-check the statement for truth (hunt small counterexamples); a false pinned statement silently blocks a whole cone and can smuggle wrong `\uses` (here it dragged a phantom `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay`/0AVF dependency into the blueprint for ~30 iterations).
- **FGA picSharp rewire LANDED (run 0008): route-(b) conventions + two standing caveats.**

State after the rewire (`Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean`, **4 real `sorry`s**, all TRUE statements — Ground-verified run 0008 reconcile):

- `picSharp C := PicSharp.relPresheaf C ⋙ forget AddCommGrpCat` (`Type (u+1)`-valued, REAL). `Scheme.HasRationalPoint C` = Nonempty section subtype of `C.hom`; the ONE genuine FGA sorry is `instHasPicScheme [HasRationalPoint C]`. The other three are typed-sorry carriers: `PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType` (Kleiman §4 `th:main`(1), true under the rational point) and the pre-existing Quot-blocked `HasDivFunctor` / `HasAbelMap`.
- `instPicSharpRepresentable` proved by `choose_spec`; `groupSchemeStructure : CommGrpObj (PicScheme C)` proved via `CommGrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` + a `Multiplicative.ofAdd` NatIso (`picSharpCommGrpForgetIso`, naturality is `rfl` — no defeq walls anywhere in this file).
- Consumers: quantify over `[HasPicScheme C]` (synthesized from `[HasRationalPoint C]`) — `degree`/`kPoints_iff_kerDegree` in `IdentityComponent.lean` now carry it. `Pic0Scheme C := GroupScheme.IdentityComponent (PicScheme C)` is now a real def (axiom-clean), gated on `PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType`.

**Caveat 1 — pointless curves:** `JacobianWitness` (`Jacobian.lean`) requires the abelian variety $J$ to exist UNconditionally, but route (b) only builds `PicScheme` for pointed $C$. For a genus-$\ge 1$ curve without a rational point the eventual `picardJacobianWitness` proof needs Galois/étale descent from a finite extension where $C$ gains a point (or a pointed restatement of the north star). Not blocking anything now — record before anyone tries to close `picardJacobianWitness` directly.

**Caveat 2 — `smoothProperQuotient` statement is WEAKER than Kleiman lm:qt and unprovable as stated:** Mathlib v4.31 has NO quasi-projectivity vocabulary, so hypothesis (2) of lm:qt is missing from the Lean statement; without it the statement is FALSE (Hironaka-type free ℤ/2 action on a smooth proper non-projective 3-fold: smooth proper equivalence relation, étale-quotient sheaf a non-scheme algebraic space). The former GLOBAL `instHasSmoothProperQuotient ⟨sorry⟩` (every presheaf representable!) was deleted this run (Ground-confirmed gone); the class is a use-site hypothesis only. Do NOT reintroduce a global instance, and do NOT try to prove the theorem as stated.
- **`pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (FBC leaf, `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean:186`) is NOT a cheap adjointness win — Ground's recommendation had the variance backwards. Route analysis (session 0013), do not re-derive.**

- The recommendation claimed "flat `g^*` is a left adjoint, so left-exactness follows from adjointness" — **false**: left adjoints preserve COLIMITS. Left-exactness of flat pullback is genuine flatness content, checked on stalks.
- No dodge exists at the presheaf level: `PresheafOfModules.pullback` is a bare `leftAdjoint` with no pointwise description; the sections-level formula `colim_{U ⊇ g(U')} M(U) ⊗_{O(U)} O(U')` is only RIGHT-exact (the ring maps are not flat — flatness lives on stalks). Sheafification + `SheafOfModules.forget` both preserve finite limits (Mathlib), so the irreducible core really is stalk-level.
- **Viable honest route** (est. 400–800 LOC of new infra): (1) combinators exist — `Functor.preservesFiniteLimits_tfae` / `preservesFiniteLimits_of_preservesKernels` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/ShortComplex/ExactFunctor.lean`); (2) Mathlib 2026 has module structure on stalks (`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Stalk.lean`, `PresheafOfModules.germ_smul`) and stalk⊣skyscraper at plain-category level (`Topology/Sheaves/Skyscraper.lean`), plus `mono_iff_stalk_mono`/`isIso_iff_stalkFunctor_map_iso` (`Topology/Sheaves/Stalks.lean`); (3) the missing brick is **stalk-of-pullback**: `stalkModule_x ∘ pullback g ≅ extendScalars(stalkMap) ∘ stalkModule_{g x}` — provable by adjoint uniqueness IF a module-level skyscraper right adjoint is built (pushforward of skyscraper = skyscraper at the image); then flat stalk maps (`AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.iff_flat_stalkMap`-adjacent, `RingHom.Flat` appLE definition) + `ModuleCat.preservesFiniteLimits_extendScalars_of_flat` (`ModuleCat/Descent.lean`) finish via kernel-comparison-on-stalks.
- **Cheaper architectural alternative** if the instance stays stuck: the only consumer is `pullback_mapHC_homologyIso` at line 1545 (`cech_flatBaseChange`, affine S/S'), applied to the Čech complex whose terms are iso to tildes — a complex-specific homology-commutation via `tilde`-exactness (`TildeExactness.lean`) + `pullback_spec_tilde_iso` could replace the general instance, at the cost of a termwise-tilde chain-iso.
- **A.3 Pic0 cone state after run-0008 T5 — supersedes the run-0005 'IdentityComponent all 9 sorries blocked' memory.**

- **Proved sorry-free**: `Pic0.grpObj` (`Nonempty (GrpObj (Pic0Scheme C))` = `isSubgroupHomomorphism (PicScheme C)`; instances synthesize directly from `groupSchemeStructure` (CommGrpObj parent projection) + the `PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType` projection instance; `Pic0Scheme` unfolds by rfl, plain `exact` works). Also `Pic0.geometricallyIrreducible` (specialisation of the substrate), both `isAbelianVariety` assemblies (the `Pic0Scheme`-namespace one MOVED to `Pic0AbelianVariety.lean`), and `tangentSpaceCotangentDual` now hypothesis-free.
- **Still blocked, do not re-probe**: `isFiniteTypeGeometricallyIrreducible` QC∧GeomIrred conjunct (EGA IV₂ 4.5.8/4.6.1 translate-cover argument, not in Mathlib v4.31); `degree`/`kPoints_iff_kerDegree` (Quot/Hilbert χ endgame); `finrank_eq_genus`, `Pic0.smooth`, `Pic0.proper` (need tangentSpaceIso resp. Chevalley–Rosenlicht).
- **tangentSpaceIso attack path opened**: the ring-level truncated-exponential splitting is DONE (`Picard/DualNumberUnits.lean`, axiom-clean: `(R[ε])ˣ ≃* Rˣ × (R,+)` + naturality via `mapRingHom`; blueprint `sec:pic0_trunc_exp`, 15 nodes). Next bricks in order: (1) dual-number points of `Pic0Scheme` ↔ `ker(picSharp(Spec k[ε]) → picSharp(Spec k))` via `(representable C).homEquiv` (clopen ⟹ Pic vs Pic⁰ dual-number points at 0 agree); (2) sheaf-level sequence on `C_ε = C ×ₖ Spec k[ε]` (identify `O_{C_ε}` sections with `O_C(U)[ε]`); (3) Čech `H¹(O^×)` cocycle classification of line bundles trivial mod ε.
- **FBC naturality leaves: precise missing layer (post-heart-closure, run 0009 T2; supersedes '4 leaf sorries' notes).** FBC has exactly **3 sorries**, all in `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean`: the two cosimplicial `naturality` fields (`cech_pushforward_baseChange_natIso` ~1690, `twisted_cech_nerve_iso` ~1758) and `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (197). `FlatBaseChange.lean` is sorry-free (mate stubs deleted).

- **The naturality fields are NOT quick wins.** Both components are ≪≫-chains through `pushPull_sigma_iso` + `Pi.mapIso` of per-σ isos. Missing layer, in order: (1) a transport formula expressing `(CechNerve 𝒰 F).map φ` through `pushPull_sigma_iso` as a matrix of restrictions `pushPullObj F (Over.mk j_σ) ⟶ pushPullObj F (Over.mk j_τ)` (only `pushPull_sigma_iso_π`/`_π_incl` exist, in `CechSectionIdentificationBase/Leg`); (2) per-σ restriction compatibility of the heavy per-σ isos (`pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChange`, `twisted_cech_nerve_per_sigma`) — coherence through the `AffinePushPullEssImage` essImage/adjoint constructions. Estimate: multi-hundred LOC each; one focused session per leaf at best.
- **Heart-closure recipe that landed** (session 0011 surgery, verified 0013): paste `restrictedCartesianAffinePushout` onto the ambient square (`IsPullback.paste_vert`), convert with the converse `isPushout_of_isPullback_SpecMap` (`IsPullback.of_map_of_faithful` on `Scheme.Spec`, then `.unop.flip`), identify the pullback corner with `coverInterOpen 𝒰' σ` via the slice iso, rewrite corner sections (`coverInterOpen_baseChange_restrictedMap_comm` + `pullbackRestrict_iso_tilde` + `pullback_spec_tilde_iso`), finish with `affinePushforwardPullbackBaseChange`. `set_option maxHeartbeats 800000` scoped per declaration suffices.
- Recipe: building ModuleCat-R morphisms into sheaf-of-modules section objects (v4.31) — the ConcreteCategory.ofHom + presheaf-obj pattern, and the let-fvar IsIso gotcha

Landed in `tildeIso_of_isQuasicoherent_isAffineOpen` (`QuotScheme.lean`, T12 run 0010). When you need `f : ModuleCat.of R M ⟶ <sections object>` (e.g. to feed `tilde.map`):

- **Target the honest `ModuleCat R` object** `(modulesSpecToSheaf.obj G).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤)` — this is literally the domain module of Mathlib's `fromTildeΓ`/`toOpen`. Do NOT target `moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj G` via `ModuleCat.ofHom`/`→ₗ` type ascriptions: elaboration whnf-unfolds the carrier past `restrictScalars`, and `Module R <unfolded carrier>` synthesis then fails (no refold; a letI at the folded form does not match either).
- **Construct via `ConcreteCategory.ofHom { toFun := ..., map_add' := ..., map_smul' := ... }`** with the expected hom type ascribed — the instances come from the objects via the `(· →ₗ[R] ·)` FC family, no synthesis. A bare `let blin : M →ₗ[R] <object> := ...` fails; skipping the standalone LinearMap and going straight to the categorical hom works.
- **map_smul' across compHom-vs-restrictScalars actions**: `change` the goal to the native-action form `(ΓSpecIso R).inv.hom r • f x`, then `rw [← hact]; exact h1` where `hact : appLE V ⊤ e = (ΓSpecIso R).inv` (proved by `rw [Scheme.Hom.appLE, fromSpec_app_self, Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, Subsingleton.elim _ (𝟙 ⊤), op_id, map_id, comp_id]`).
- **IsIso of `tilde.map b'` for a `let`-bound b'**: `haveI : IsIso (tilde.map b') := by rw [show tilde.map b' = (tilde.functor _).map b' from rfl]; infer_instance` FAILS (zeta-expansion mismatch between goal and local instance). Use `inferInstanceAs (IsIso ((tilde.functor _).map b'))` — works even without `clear_value`.
- **Unit-component bijectivity at open immersions**: `Adjunction.unit_leftAdjointUniq_hom_app (restrictAdjunction j) (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction j) N` + `restrictAdjunction_unit_app_app` (plain presheaf restriction along `j''ᵁj⁻¹ᵁV = V`) + component of `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`. When `rw [← AddCommGrpCat.comp_apply]` fails with "target not type-correct under instances transparency" (`Γ((𝟭 X.Modules).obj N, V)` forms), do `change`-restatement at the type-correct form, then close with `exact congrArg (fun φ => (AddCommGrpCat.Hom.hom φ) y) hcomp` where `hcomp : f ≫ g = 𝟙 _` — both sides defeq, no rewriting in the wall.
- **Σ-pair trace**: `tilde.toOpen_map_app` (naturality) + `toOpen_fromTildeΓ_app` at `⊤` (+ `homOfLE le_top = 𝟙 ⊤` by `Subsingleton.elim`) give `(tilde.map b' ≫ fromTildeΓ).app ⊤ ∘ toOpen = b'` elementwise, via `congrArg`-chains only.
- **Recipes: module-level dévissage in Lean v4.31 (landed in FlatteningStratification GenericFreeness, run 0010 T12 r2) — reuse for any prime-filtration/SES argument.**

- **ModuleCat SES arguments need ONE universe**: `ModuleCat.ofHom {X Y : Type v}` forces all modules of a `ShortComplex (ModuleCat R)` into the same universe. Binders `{M₁ M₂ M₃ : Type*}` give distinct universes and produce baffling failures — instance goals displayed as raw `Quot ⇑(OreLocalization.oreEqv ...)` (LocalizedModule unfolded past its instance heads) plus universe-mismatched `Eq.{max u_1 u_4}` vs `Eq.{max u_1 u_2}`. Pin `Type v`. The dévissage eliminator is single-universe anyway.
- **`set S := Submonoid.powers (f*g)` breaks instance unification** in localized-module types (same family as the known let-fvar IsIso gotcha): instance search will not zeta-unfold the set-fvar when unifying `Localization S'` heads. Write the submonoid term explicitly everywhere; `let`-bound MAPS (data) are fine — only types/instances mind.
- **Quantified-instance motive** for `IsNoetherianRing.induction_on_isQuotientEquivQuotientPrime` when the property lives over a DIFFERENT ring A than the induction ring B: `motive N _ iMB _ := ∀ (iA : Module A N), @IsScalarTower A B N Algebra.toSMul iMB.toDistribMulAction.toMulAction.toSMul iA.toDistribMulAction.toMulAction.toSMul → @P A N _ _ iA`. Cases: subsingleton via @-application; quotient case transports along `(e.restrictScalars A).symm` (canonical tower on B⧸p is instance-found); exact case builds `letI iA₁ := Module.compHom N₁ (algebraMap A B)` + tower `⟨fun a b n => by change (a • b) • n = algebraMap A B a • b • n; rw [Algebra.smul_def, mul_smul]⟩`; B-linear maps restrict via `LinearMap.restrictScalars A` and inj/surj/Exact transfer definitionally. Final application `... ‹Module A M› ‹IsScalarTower A B M›` unifies. Compiled essentially first-try.
- **Two-localization freeness transport** (`M_f` free ⟹ `M_{fg}` free) WITHOUT IsLocalizedModule-composition: `(IsLocalization.Away.awayToAwayRight f g).toAlgebra` + `IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq (awayToAwayRight_eq ..).symm`, then equiv-chain `congr(refl, isBaseChange f) .symm ≪≫ cancelBaseChange ≪≫ isBaseChange (f*g)` and `Module.Free.tensor` + `Module.Free.of_equiv'`. Give every LinearEquiv a fully-typed `have` — `refine LinearEquiv.trans ?_ e` with open source type sticks on RingHomCompTriple metavariables.
- **Localized SES**: `IsLocalizedModule.map/map_injective/map_surjective/map_exact` at `mkLinearMap`s, upgrade R-linear→Loc-linear with `LinearMap.extendScalarsOfIsLocalization` (coe unchanged, so Function.Exact/Injective/Surjective transfer by defeq); `ShortComplex.mk (ofHom u') (ofHom v') (ModuleCat.hom_ext (LinearMap.ext fun x => hexact.apply_apply_eq_zero x))`; ShortExact via `moduleCat_exact_iff` + `mono_iff_injective` + `epi_iff_surjective`; finish `ModuleCat.free_shortExact`.

The genus split is *already* mostly retired: `Genus0BaseObjects.lean`, `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`, and `genusZeroWitness`/`positiveGenusWitness` are deleted; `Jacobian C` is uniform via `picardJacobianWitness`. The remaining T13 work is residue cleanup, with one dependency trap.

**Trap — `AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean` serves two masters.**

- Its `Scheme.PrimeDivisor` type and `Scheme.RationalMap.order : X.functionField → ℤ` valuation are the codim-1 substrate that `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` (`ALB.codim1`, live north-star path) imports and uses — the `coheight = 1` convention (`CodimOneExtension.lean:177`) and the order-valuation appeal (`:1823`).

**Consequence:** a wholesale delete of `WeilDivisor.lean` breaks the codim-1 extension build. The dependency-safe shape is a surgical carve of the RR/degree/ℙ¹ portion plus the file header (which frames the whole file around `genusZero_curve_iso_P1`), retaining the `PrimeDivisor`/`order` substrate.

**Also live, not genus-0:** `RigidityLemma.lean` and blueprint `AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex` are the sorry-free rigidity-lemma + Milne §I.1–I.3 corollaries used by the uniform Albanese construction; only their "feed the genus-0 base case (Route C)" prose is stale — clean the prose, keep the math.
- **T12 r-current: hilbertPolynomial headline def CLOSED (real definition) — updates the 'QuotScheme 8 sorries' triage of I-0089.**

- New file `Picard/HilbertPolynomial.lean` (~230 LOC, axiom-clean `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`, LSP + full `lake build` green, 8647 jobs): `Scheme.hilbertPolynomial` is now REAL — the unique `Φ ∈ ℚ[λ]` eventually matching the graded Hilbert function `m ↦ dim_{κ(s)} Γ(X_s, F_s ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})` (`Scheme.hilbertFunction`), junk `0` outside the regime. Fibre = Mathlib `Scheme.Hom.fiber`; restrictions via `Scheme.Modules.pullback (π.fiberι s)` (`Hom.fiberModule`); twists via `SectionGradedRing.moduleTensorPow`; κ(s)-structure = `Module.compHom` along `Hom.fiberResidueMap` (`ΓSpecIso.inv ≫ (fiberToSpecResidueField).appTop`) — all compiled essentially first-try, no defeq walls.

- **QuotScheme.lean now 7 sorries** (4 headline: QuotFunctor/Grassmannian/Grassmannian.representable/QuotScheme + 3 FBC-gated base-change leaves). QuotScheme.lean now imports HilbertPolynomial (which imports SectionGradedRing + GradedHilbertSerre; both were previously leaf files — no cycle, verified).
- **Remaining Hilbert lane, in order**: (1) `lem:sectionGradedModule_fg` — Serre finite-generation of `⊕_m Γ(X_s, F_s ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})` over `⊕_m Γ(X_s, L_s^{⊗m})` for proper X_s + ample L_s, THE deep remaining input (needs ampleness vocabulary; multi-session); (2) the DirectSum-decomposition bridge instantiating `gradedModule_hilbertSeries_rational` at the fibre section module (external ⊕ → internal ℳ decomposition + degree-1 commuting endos from a κ(s)-basis of R₁ + Module.Finite over MvPolynomial); (3) then `QuotFunctor` def can consume `hilbertPolynomial` (its statement needs no new theory — flatness/coherence predicates + Setoid quotient + pullback functoriality, one focused session).

Previously-dangling `lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen` now has its `\label` (`:5255`). Related: [[I-0075]] (pullback-qcoh 01BG), [[I-0113]], [[I-0109]].

This is a ONE-SHOT headless session: when you produce your final report the session ENDS and you will NOT be re-invoked. Nothing calls you back when a background job finishes, and any background process you started is killed when the session exits. So you cannot 'kick off a build and wait to be notified' — that notification never comes here, and the build dies.
Therefore: if a build, subagent, monitor, or shell command produces a result your conclusion depends on, you MUST get that result WITHIN this session — run it in the FOREGROUND and block on it (read its exit code), or actively poll until it has definitively finished, before writing the report. A heavy `lake build` can take many minutes; budget for that and wait it out. If it genuinely cannot finish in time, do NOT imply success: record the unfinished build under `## Issues`, state what's still unknown, and leave the explicit next action — never write a conclusion that assumes a pending result.

Finish with a brief run-local report. Recommended sections: `## Summary`, `## Progress`, `## Issues`, `## Why I stopped`, and `## Next`; add or rename other sections if another shape is clearer, but keep `## Progress` and `## Why I stopped`. In `## Progress`, use only inline `-` bullets, one per relevant file or target, e.g. `- FileA.lean: 4 sorries -> 3 sorries; closed the base case.` or `- FileB.lean: No change because dependency X is blocked.` Do not use nested bullets or wrapped multi-line bullets there. In `## Why I stopped`, start with either `Task complete:` when the assigned objective is genuinely complete, or `Task not complete:` when work remains. If there is a plausible next action inside the session's scope and budget, take it before stopping; a clean commit or green partial check is not by itself a reason to stop. Prefer short bullets, and keep each bullet around 20 words or fewer. Always mention bugs, build failures, broken proofs, suspicious code issues, blocked dependencies, unresolved assumptions, and checks that failed or were not run. For this {role} session, make the report complete enough that a human or Ground can understand the session before opening raw logs.

name = "AlgebraicJacobian"
version = "0.1.0"
keywords = ["math"]
defaultTargets = ["AlgebraicJacobian"]
packagesDir = "../../.lake-packages"


[leanOptions]
pp.unicode.fun = true # pretty-prints `fun a ↦ b`
relaxedAutoImplicit = false
weak.linter.mathlibStandardSet = true
maxSynthPendingDepth = 3

[[require]]
name = "checkdecls"
git = "https://github.com/PatrickMassot/checkdecls.git"

[[require]]
name = "«doc-gen4»"
git = "https://github.com/leanprover/doc-gen4"
# pinned to the doc-gen4 commit on main that targets toolchain v4.31.0
# (rev "main" tracks v4.32.0-rc1 and conflicts with mathlib v4.31.0)
rev = "0bc516c1b9db"

# mathlib required LAST so its transitive dep versions (e.g. Cli) take
# precedence over doc-gen4's, keeping `lake exe cache get` hashes correct.
[[require]]
name = "mathlib"
scope = "leanprover-community"
rev = "v4.31.0"

[[lean_lib]]
name = "AlgebraicJacobian"

export const meta = {
  name: 'T14-substrate-inventory',
  description: 'Parallel signature inventory of Mathlib Proj/morphism-class substrate and project gluing/graded-ring engines for the T14 projectivity foundation',
  phases: [{ title: 'Inventory', detail: 'parallel readers over Mathlib and project files' }],
}

const SCHEMA = {
  type: 'object',
  properties: {
    summary: { type: 'string' },
    declarations: {
      type: 'array',
      items: {
        type: 'object',
        properties: {
          name: { type: 'string' },
          signature: { type: 'string' },
          file: { type: 'string' },
          line: { type: 'number' },
          notes: { type: 'string' },
        },
        required: ['name', 'signature', 'file'],
      },
    },
    pitfalls: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' } },
  },
  required: ['summary', 'declarations', 'pitfalls'],
}

const MATHLIB = '/home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/.lake-packages/mathlib'
const PROJ = '/home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge'

const READERS = [
  {
    key: 'proj-scheme',
    prompt: `You are inventorying Lean 4 Mathlib (v4.31) source for a formalization session. Read these files carefully:
${MATHLIB}/Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/ProjectiveSpectrum/Scheme.lean
${MATHLIB}/Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/ProjectiveSpectrum/Basic.lean
${MATHLIB}/Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/ProjectiveSpectrum/Proper.lean

Report EXACT declaration names, full signatures (copy the source), file, line number for:
1. The definition of AlgebraicGeometry.Proj (as a Scheme) and its namespace; what graded-ring assumptions/variables it takes (universe, CommRing A, GradedAlgebra/GradedRing 𝒜, 𝒜 : ℕ → Submodule R A?).
2. Proj.toSpecZero — exact statement, and ALL instances proved about it in Proper.lean: IsProper, LocallyOfFiniteType, IsSeparated, UniversallyClosed, quasi-compact — with their exact hypotheses (e.g. [Algebra.FiniteType (𝒜 0) A] or finite generation in degree 1?).
3. Proj.awayι, Proj.basicOpen / the affine open cover of Proj by basic opens D+(f) — exact names and signatures (Proj.affineOpenCover? Proj.openCoverOfISupEqTop?), including how the away-algebra HomogeneousLocalization.Away appears.
4. Proj.fromOfGlobalSections and any lemma about maps into Proj.
5. Anything about O(1), twisted structure sheaf, Serre twist, 'twistedStructureSheaf', 'tautological' — search the whole ProjectiveSpectrum directory for these.
Also grep the ProjectiveSpectrum directory listing and report any other files there (e.g. Functor.lean, StructureSheaf.lean) with one-line contents summary.`,
    effort: 'medium',
  },
  {
    key: 'affinespace-pattern',
    prompt: `You are inventorying Lean 4 Mathlib (v4.31) source. Read ${MATHLIB}/Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/AffineSpace.lean fully.
Report EXACT names/signatures/lines for: the definition AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace (how it is defined — pullback over Spec ℤ? functor?), its structural morphism to the base S, functoriality (map along S' ⟶ S), base-change isos (AffineSpace over pullback), the universal property (hom equiv with sections/functions), and every instance proved about the structural morphism. Also report the exact mechanism used to say 'Spec ℤ is terminal' (specZIsTerminal? terminalIsTerminal?) and how terminal.from appears. Copy the key definitional lines verbatim. Also check ${MATHLIB}/Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Limits.lean for: terminal object of Scheme (exact name of the instance/def identifying it with Spec ℤ), existence of pullbacks/finite limits in Scheme, and names like Scheme.terminalIsoSpecZ.`,
    effort: 'medium',
  },
  {
    key: 'morphism-classes',
    prompt: `You are inventorying Lean 4 Mathlib (v4.31) source at ${MATHLIB}. Inventory the scheme-morphism property classes needed to prove stability facts (base change, composition) for a new 'projective morphism' predicate:
1. AlgebraicGeometry.IsProper — file Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Morphisms/Proper.lean: exact class definition, instances for composition (comp), base change stability (IsStableUnderBaseChange / pullback_fst / pullback_snd instances), closed immersions are proper (where?), IsProper respects isos.
2. AlgebraicGeometry.IsClosedImmersion — Morphisms/ClosedImmersion.lean: class, stability under base change (exact lemma/instance names), composition, IsClosedImmersion of pullback.map / pullback.lift, and 'IsClosedImmersion → IsProper' route (via IsFinite? universally closed + separated + finite type?). Report the exact chain that exists.
3. LocallyOfFiniteType, IsSeparated, QuasiCompact — comp + base-change instance names.
4. The MorphismProperty machinery as used in AlgebraicGeometry: how one typically derives 'instance : IsProper (pullback.snd f g)' from 'IsProper g' — exact lemma names (e.g. MorphismProperty.pullback_snd, IsStableUnderBaseChange.snd, AlgebraicGeometry.IsProper.stableUnderBaseChange?). Copy signatures verbatim with file:line.
5. Also: HasAffineProperty/IsAffineHom if relevant, and 'IsPreimmersion'/'IsImmersion' only if trivially nearby. And check whether any 'Projective' or 'QuasiProjective' morphism class exists ANYWHERE under Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry (grep -ri 'projective' on Morphisms/ and report).`,
    effort: 'medium',
  },
  {
    key: 'mvpoly-graded',
    prompt: `You are inventorying Lean 4 Mathlib (v4.31) source at ${MATHLIB}. Report exact names/signatures/lines for:
1. MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule and the GradedAlgebra instance (Mathlib/RingTheory/MvPolynomial/Homogeneous.lean) — exact instance name and its index type (ℕ?), and over what base (CommSemiring R).
2. What is (MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule σ R 0) — degree-0 part: any lemma identifying it with constants / R (e.g. homogeneousSubmodule_zero? isHomogeneous iff?). Search for lemmas about degree-zero homogeneous polynomials being C r.
3. Algebra.FiniteType instances for MvPolynomial over finite variable sets (MvPolynomial.instFiniteType? Algebra.FiniteType.mvPolynomial), and how one would get Algebra.FiniteType (𝒜 0) A when 𝒜 0 is a subring of A = MvPolynomial (Fin n) R with R = ℤ or a CommRing. Look for 'GradedRing.instAlgebraFiniteType' or similar, and for the subring/subalgebra 'SetLike.GradeZero' algebra instances (Mathlib/Algebra/GradedAlgebra/Basic.lean? SetLike/GradedRing homogeneous elements): exact mechanism giving CommRing (𝒜 0) and Algebra (𝒜 0) A.
4. GradedRing.finiteType-style lemmas: anything asserting a graded ring finitely generated over degree-0 (search 'GradedRing' + 'FiniteType', 'Finite' in RingTheory/GradedAlgebra/).
5. HomogeneousLocalization: the Away structure (HomogeneousLocalization.Away), its CommRing structure, and lemmas used by Proj charts.`,
    effort: 'medium',
  },
  {
    key: 'glue-engine',
    prompt: `You are inventorying the project's module-gluing engine so a new Serre-twist sheaf O(m) on Proj can be glued from local data. Read:
${PROJ}/AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/GlueDescent.lean
${PROJ}/AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/GrassmannianZariskiSheaf.lean (the parts that USE the gluing engine)
and grep the whole ${PROJ}/AlgebraicJacobian tree for 'Modules.glue' / 'Scheme.Modules.glue' and read its defining file.
Report: (a) the EXACT entry-point signature(s) for gluing a SheafOfModules over a Scheme from a cover + local sheaves + transition isos + cocycle condition — copy verbatim with file:line; (b) what shape the cover must have (Scheme.OpenCover? opens? IsOpenImmersion legs?); (c) what the transition data type is; (d) what compatibility/cocycle fields must be provided; (e) any worked example of gluing LINE bundles / rank-1 free modules over basic opens (GrassmannianZariskiSheaf or elsewhere) that could be imitated for O(1) with transitions t_i/t_j; (f) how glued-sheaf sections/restriction to a cover leg are recovered (iso to the local piece). Also report the SheafOfModules basics the project uses: Scheme.Modules category name (X.Modules?), pullback functor name (Scheme.Modules.pullback?), unit object, tensor. List pitfalls noted in comments.`,
    effort: 'medium',
  },
  {
    key: 'graded-ring-project',
    prompt: `You are inventorying project files defining the section graded ring/module for the Serre-finiteness statement. Read:
${PROJ}/AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/SectionGradedRing.lean (fully)
${PROJ}/AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/HilbertPolynomial.lean (fully)
Report exact names/signatures/lines for: SectionGradedRing (the graded ring ⊕ Γ(X, L^⊗m)), the graded module ⊕ Γ(X, F ⊗ L^⊗m) (sectionGradedModule?), SectionGradedRing.moduleTensorPow, Scheme.hilbertFunction, Scheme.hilbertPolynomial, and how κ(s)-module structures are put on sections (Module.compHom along which map). What EXACTLY would 'sectionGradedModule_fg' need to say to be consumable: over which ring (the graded ring as a plain ring? via DirectSum?), which finiteness class (Module.Finite? Algebra.FiniteType?). Report how gradedModule_hilbertSeries_rational in ${PROJ}/AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/GradedHilbertSerre.lean is stated (read its statement + hypotheses only, not proofs) and what bridge inputs it expects. Also list what ample/very-ample vocabulary if any appears anywhere in the project (grep -ri 'ample' ${PROJ}/AlgebraicJacobian --include=*.lean).`,
    effort: 'medium',
  },
  {
    key: 'blueprint-nodes',
    prompt: `You are inventorying blueprint LaTeX for coordination. Read ${PROJ}/blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex around lines 1-700 and 5000-5600, plus grep for 'sectionGradedModule_fg', 'quot_boundedness', 'very ample', 'ample', 'projective', 'P(n', 'O(1)', 'Serre' in ALL files under ${PROJ}/blueprint/src/chapters/.
Report: (a) the full LaTeX source of the node lem:sectionGradedModule_fg (statement + proof + \\uses + \\lean pins) — copy verbatim; (b) same for lem:quot_boundedness; (c) any existing blueprint nodes about projective space, O(1), ampleness, projective morphisms (list labels + \\lean pins); (d) the sectioning structure of Picard_QuotScheme.tex (list \\section/\\subsection titles with line numbers) so new nodes can be placed; (e) which nodes currently carry \\leanok vs not in the Serre-finiteness neighborhood; (f) the house conventions visible in this file for \\lean{...} \\leanok \\uses \\source citations (copy 2 representative complete nodes). Also read ${PROJ}/blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_SectionGradedRing.tex section headers.`,
    effort: 'medium',
  },
  {
    key: 'proj-functor-basechange',
    prompt: `You are inventorying Lean 4 Mathlib (v4.31) source at ${MATHLIB}. Read Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/ProjectiveSpectrum/Functor.lean (if present; else find Proj functoriality elsewhere) and report: Proj.map signature and hypotheses (graded ring homs? surjective? degree-preserving), Proj.mapIso, whether a CLOSED IMMERSION criterion for Proj.map of a surjective graded hom exists (grep 'IsClosedImmersion' in ProjectiveSpectrum/). Also search Mathlib for: (1) 'Segre' embedding (2) closed immersion into projective space lemmas (3) any 'IsAmple'/'VeryAmple'/'ample' declarations ANYWHERE (grep -ri ample Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry Mathlib/Geometry 2>/dev/null; also Mathlib/RingTheory for 'ample') (4) 'Conrad' or relative Proj: grep -ri 'relative proj\\|RelativeProj\\|Proj.relative' Mathlib/. Report exact hits with file:line and signatures, or 'ABSENT' verdicts. Finally check Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Morphisms/ directory listing and report ALL files (names only) so we know the full morphism-class vocabulary available.`,
    effort: 'medium',
  },
]

phase('Inventory')
const results = await parallel(READERS.map(r => () =>
  agent(r.prompt, { label: `read:${r.key}`, phase: 'Inventory', schema: SCHEMA, effort: r.effort })
))

const out = {}
READERS.forEach((r, i) => { out[r.key] = results[i] })
return out

input.description: Read I-0118 and I-0109 full bodies and comments

data.content:
==================== I-0118 issue 
QuotScheme statement is unprovable-as-pinned: properness alone is not the Nitsure theorem

The pinned AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotScheme (QuotFunctorDef.lean, final theorem) hypothesizes only [IsProper pi] [LocallyOfFiniteType pi] with arbitrary quasi-coherent L and arbitrary E, but the source theorem ([Nitsure] Sec. 5, thm:quot_representable) requires pi projective, L relatively very ample, E coherent. For merely proper X the Quot/Hilbert functor is in general only an algebraic space (Hironaka's smooth proper non-projective 3-fold is the standard counterexample family), so the pinned statement is false-or-open, not the cited theorem. Same trap genus as the old Grassmannian.representable skeleton corrected this session (GrassmannianRepresentability.lean, thm:grassmannian_representable NOTE). Next action before attacking this sorry: design the projectivity / relative-very-ampleness encoding (absent from Mathlib v4.31, see I-0109 ampleness gap), restate faithfully, then prove. Do NOT close the sorry as stated.
--- comment C-0001 by ground
**Blueprint side reconciled (session 0109-ground).** The node `thm:quot_representable` (`Picard_QuotScheme.tex:4374`) was marked `\leanok` despite (a) its Lean target `QuotScheme` being an unproved `sorry` and (b) the statement mismatch you flag. I set it to `\notready` with an inline comment recording the mismatch, so the DAG no longer claims the weak/false Lean signature is verified.

- The **blueprint statement is correct** (projective $\pi$, relatively very ample $L$, coherent $E$ — matches Nitsure §5). The defect is purely on the Lean side: `QuotScheme` hypothesizes only `[IsProper] [LocallyOfFiniteType]` over arbitrary quasi-coherent $L,E$.
- Per memory `I-0074` caveat 2, Mathlib v4.31 has no projectivity/very-ampleness vocabulary, so the Lean cannot yet be restated to match — the honest move is to leave `QuotScheme` unproved (or re-scope it) rather than prove the false pinned form.
--- comment C-0002 by human
**Restatement design settled** (T12 ultracode session triage; Mathlib v4.31 vocabulary hunt done — NO IsProjective/QuasiProjective morphism class, NO VeryAmple/IsAmple for sheaves, NO relative Proj or $\mathbb{P}^n_S$, NO $\mathcal{O}(1)$ as a sheaf of modules anywhere in Mathlib).

Recommended faithful encoding (a): build the missing vocabulary block project-side —
- $\mathbb{P}(n;S) :=$ `pullback (terminal.from S) (terminal.from (Proj (MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin n) ℤ)))`, in the style of Mathlib's `AffineSpace` (`AffineSpace.lean:44`). Properness of the structural map comes from Mathlib's `IsProper (Proj.toSpecZero 𝒜)` (`ProjectiveSpectrum/Proper.lean:368`) + base change.
- Serre twist $\mathcal{O}(1)$ as a `SheafOfModules` on `Proj 𝒜`, glued over the `awayι`/basic-open affine cover with transitions $t_i/t_j$ — the project's `GlueDescent`/`GrassmannianZariskiSheaf` module-gluing engine is the substrate.
- Then restate: `theorem QuotScheme … (hE : E.IsFinitePresentation) (emb : ∃ n (i : X ⟶ ℙ(n;S)), IsClosedImmersion i ∧ i ≫ proj = π ∧ Nonempty (L ≅ i^* 𝒪(1)))` and flip `thm:quot_representable` from \notready.

Fallback encoding (c) for the sole Route-A consumer ($S = \operatorname{Spec} k$): very-ampleness through the project's own `SectionGradedRing` $\bigoplus_m \Gamma(X, L^{\otimes m})$ + Mathlib `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` (`ProjectiveSpectrum/Basic.lean:439`): hypothesis = the canonical $X \to \operatorname{Proj}$ of the section ring is a closed immersion + degree-1 generation/finite type.

Encoding (b) (a class packaging the Nitsure-consumed consequences — m-regularity bounds, pushforward local freeness) is REJECTED as the public statement: conclusion-carrier of the `HasSmoothProperQuotient` genus, statement-drift magnet; acceptable only as a use-site hypothesis.

Next action: dedicated vocabulary-build session for $\mathbb{P}(n;S)$ + $\mathcal{O}(1)$, then the restatement. Until then `Scheme.QuotScheme` (QuotFunctorDef.lean:753) keeps its sorry.
==================== I-0109 memory 
**T12 r-current: hilbertPolynomial headline def CLOSED (real definition) — updates the 'QuotScheme 8 sorries' triage of I-0089.**

- New file `Picard/HilbertPolynomial.lean` (~230 LOC, axiom-clean `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`, LSP + full `lake build` green, 8647 jobs): `Scheme.hilbertPolynomial` is now REAL — the unique `Φ ∈ ℚ[λ]` eventually matching the graded Hilbert function `m ↦ dim_{κ(s)} Γ(X_s, F_s ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})` (`Scheme.hilbertFunction`), junk `0` outside the regime. Fibre = Mathlib `Scheme.Hom.fiber`; restrictions via `Scheme.Modules.pullback (π.fiberι s)` (`Hom.fiberModule`); twists via `SectionGradedRing.moduleTensorPow`; κ(s)-structure = `Module.compHom` along `Hom.fiberResidueMap` (`ΓSpecIso.inv ≫ (fiberToSpecResidueField).appTop`) — all compiled essentially first-try, no defeq walls.

13	/-!
14	# Affine space
15	
16	## Main definitions
17	
18	- `AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace`: `𝔸(n; S)` is the affine `n`-space over `S`.
19	- `AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.coord`: The standard coordinate functions on the affine space.
20	- `AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.homOfVector`:
21	  The morphism `X ⟶ 𝔸(n; S)` given by a `X ⟶ S` and a choice of `n`-coordinate functions.
22	- `AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.homOverEquiv`:
23	  `S`-morphisms into `Spec 𝔸(n; S)` are equivalent to the choice of `n` global sections.
24	- `AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.SpecIso`: `𝔸(n; Spec R) ≅ Spec R[n]`
25	
26	-/
27	
28	@[expose] public section
29	
30	open CategoryTheory Limits MvPolynomial
31	
32	noncomputable section
33	
34	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
35	
36	universe u
37	
38	variable (n : Type u) (S : Scheme.{u})
39	
40	local notation3 "ℤ[" n "]" => CommRingCat.of (MvPolynomial n (ULift ℤ))
41	
42	/-- `𝔸(n; S)` is the affine `n`-space over `S`.
43	Note that `n : Type u` is an arbitrary index type (e.g. `ULift.{u} (Fin m)`). -/
44	def AffineSpace (n : Type u) (S : Scheme.{u}) : Scheme.{u} :=
45	  pullback (terminal.from S) (terminal.from (Spec ℤ[n]))
46	
47	namespace AffineSpace
48	
49	/-- `𝔸(n; S)` is the affine `n`-space over `S`. -/
50	scoped[AlgebraicGeometry] notation "𝔸(" n "; " S ")" => AffineSpace n S
51	
52	variable {n} in
53	lemma of_mvPolynomial_int_ext {R} {f g : ℤ[n] ⟶ R} (h : ∀ i, f (.X i) = g (.X i)) : f = g := by
54	  suffices f.hom.comp (MvPolynomial.mapEquiv _ ULift.ringEquiv.symm).toRingHom =
55	      g.hom.comp (MvPolynomial.mapEquiv _ ULift.ringEquiv.symm).toRingHom by
56	    ext x
57	    · obtain ⟨x⟩ := x
58	      simpa [-map_intCast, -eq_intCast] using! DFunLike.congr_fun this (C x)
59	    · simpa [-map_intCast, -eq_intCast] using! DFunLike.congr_fun this (X x)
60	  ext1
61	  · exact RingHom.ext_int _ _
62	  · simpa using! h _
63	
64	
65	@[simps -isSimp]
66	instance over : 𝔸(n; S).CanonicallyOver S where
67	  hom := pullback.fst _ _
68	
69	/-- The map from the affine `n`-space over `S` to the integral model `Spec ℤ[n]`. -/
70	def toSpecMvPoly : 𝔸(n; S) ⟶ Spec ℤ[n] := pullback.snd _ _
71	
72	set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
73	/--
74	Morphisms into `Spec ℤ[n]` are equivalent the choice of `n` global sections.
75	Use `homOverEquiv` instead.
76	-/
77	@[simps]
78	def toSpecMvPolyIntEquiv {X : Scheme.{u}} : (X ⟶ Spec ℤ[n]) ≃ (n → Γ(X, ⊤)) where
79	  toFun f i := f.appTop ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso ℤ[n]).inv (.X i))
80	  invFun v := X.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map
81	    (CommRingCat.ofHom (MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom ((algebraMap ℤ _).comp ULift.ringEquiv.toRingHom) v))
82	  left_inv f := by
83	    apply (ΓSpec.adjunction.homEquiv _ _).symm.injective
84	    apply Quiver.Hom.unop_inj
85	    rw [Adjunction.homEquiv_symm_apply, Adjunction.homEquiv_symm_apply]
86	    dsimp
87	    simp only [Scheme.toSpecΓ_appTop, Scheme.ΓSpecIso_naturality, Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc]
88	    apply of_mvPolynomial_int_ext
89	    intro i
90	    rw [ConcreteCategory.hom_ofHom, coe_eval₂Hom, eval₂_X]
91	    rfl
92	  right_inv v := by ext; simp
93	
94	lemma toSpecMvPolyIntEquiv_comp {X Y : Scheme} (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Spec ℤ[n]) (i) :
95	    toSpecMvPolyIntEquiv n (f ≫ g) i = f.appTop (toSpecMvPolyIntEquiv n g i) := rfl
96	
97	variable {n} in
98	/-- The standard coordinates of `𝔸(n; S)`. -/
99	def coord (i : n) : Γ(𝔸(n; S), ⊤) := toSpecMvPolyIntEquiv _ (toSpecMvPoly n S) i
100	
101	section homOfVector
102	
103	variable {n S}
104	
105	/-- The morphism `X ⟶ 𝔸(n; S)` given by a `X ⟶ S` and a choice of `n`-coordinate functions. -/
106	def homOfVector {X : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ S) (v : n → Γ(X, ⊤)) : X ⟶ 𝔸(n; S) :=
107	  pullback.lift f ((toSpecMvPolyIntEquiv n).symm v) (by simp)
108	
109	variable {X : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ S) (v : n → Γ(X, ⊤))
110	
111	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
112	lemma homOfVector_over : homOfVector f v ≫ 𝔸(n; S) ↘ S = f :=
113	  pullback.lift_fst _ _ _
114	
115	@[reassoc]
116	lemma homOfVector_toSpecMvPoly :
117	    homOfVector f v ≫ toSpecMvPoly n S = (toSpecMvPolyIntEquiv n).symm v :=
118	  pullback.lift_snd _ _ _
119	
120	@[simp]

/-!
# The Hilbert polynomial of a coherent sheaf along a fibre (Nitsure §1)

This file gives the **real definition** of the Hilbert polynomial
`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.hilbertPolynomial` of a sheaf of modules `F` on
`X ⟶ S` at a point `s : S` relative to a line bundle `L`, via the graded
Hilbert function of the fibre section module — the `H⁰`-only encoding pinned
in the blueprint (`def:hilbert_polynomial`,
`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`):

* the fibre `X_s = π.fiber s` is Mathlib's scheme-theoretic fibre
  `pullback π (S.fromSpecResidueField s)`;
* the restrictions `F_s`, `L_s` are the module pullbacks along the fibre
  embedding `π.fiberι s` (`Scheme.Hom.fiberModule`);
* the twists `F_s ⊗ L_s^{⊗m}` are `Scheme.Modules.moduleTensorPow` of
  `AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.SectionGradedRing`;
* their global sections are `κ(s)`-vector spaces via the structural ring
  homomorphism `κ(s) →+* Γ(X_s, 𝒪)` (`Scheme.Hom.fiberResidueMap`,
  `Scheme.Hom.fiberSectionsModule`), giving the graded Hilbert function
  `m ↦ dim_{κ(s)} Γ(X_s, F_s ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})` (`Scheme.hilbertFunction`);
* `Scheme.hilbertPolynomial π L F s` is the unique `Φ ∈ ℚ[λ]` that agrees

@[simp]
lemma reindex_appTop_coord {n m : Type u} (i : m → n) (S : Scheme.{u}) (j : m) :
    (reindex i S).appTop (coord S j) = coord S (i j) :=
  homOfVector_appTop_coord _ _ _

@[simp]
lemma reindex_id : reindex id S = 𝟙 𝔸(n; S) := by
  ext1 <;> simp

@[simp, reassoc]
lemma reindex_comp {n₁ n₂ n₃ : Type u} (i : n₁ ⟶ n₂) (j : n₂ ⟶ n₃) (S : Scheme.{u}) :
    reindex (i ≫ j) S = reindex j S ≫ reindex i S := by
  ext k <;> simp

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
lemma map_reindex {n₁ n₂ : Type u} (i : n₁ → n₂) {S T : Scheme.{u}} (f : S ⟶ T) :
    map n₂ f ≫ reindex i T = reindex i S ≫ map n₁ f := by
  apply hom_ext <;> simp

/-- The affine space as a functor. -/
@[simps]
def functor : (Type u)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Scheme.{u} ⥤ Scheme.{u} where
  obj n := { obj := AffineSpace n.unop, map := map n.unop, map_id := map_id, map_comp := map_comp }
  map {n m} i := { app := reindex i.unop, naturality := fun _ _ ↦ map_reindex i.unop }
  map_id n := by ext : 2; exact reindex_id _
  map_comp f g := by ext : 2; dsimp; exact reindex_comp _ _ _

end functorial
section instances

instance : IsAffineHom (𝔸(n; S) ↘ S) := MorphismProperty.pullback_fst _ _ inferInstance

instance : Surjective (𝔸(n; S) ↘ S) := MorphismProperty.pullback_fst _ _ <| by
  have := isIso_of_isTerminal specULiftZIsTerminal terminalIsTerminal (terminal.from _)
  rw [← terminal.comp_from (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom C)),
    MorphismProperty.cancel_right_of_respectsIso (P := @Surjective)]
  exact ⟨MvPolynomial.comap_C_surjective⟩

instance [Finite n] : LocallyOfFinitePresentation (𝔸(n; S) ↘ S) :=
  MorphismProperty.pullback_fst _ _ <| by
  have := isIso_of_isTerminal specULiftZIsTerminal.{u} terminalIsTerminal (terminal.from _)
  rw [← terminal.comp_from (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom C)),
    MorphismProperty.cancel_right_of_respectsIso (P := @LocallyOfFinitePresentation),
    HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff (P := @LocallyOfFinitePresentation), RingHom.FinitePresentation]
  convert! (inferInstance : Algebra.FinitePresentation (ULift ℤ) ℤ[n])
  exact Algebra.algebra_ext _ _ fun _ ↦ rfl

lemma isOpenMap_over : IsOpenMap (𝔸(n; S) ↘ S) := by
  change topologically @IsOpenMap _
  wlog hS : ∃ R, S = Spec R
  · refine (IsZariskiLocalAtTarget.iff_of_openCover
      (P := topologically @IsOpenMap) S.affineCover).mpr ?_
    intro i
    have := this (n := n) (S.affineCover.X i) ⟨_, rfl⟩
    rwa [← (isPullback_map (n := n) (S.affineCover.f i)).isoPullback_hom_snd,
      MorphismProperty.cancel_left_of_respectsIso (P := topologically @IsOpenMap)] at this
  obtain ⟨R, rfl⟩ := hS
  rw [← MorphismProperty.cancel_left_of_respectsIso (P := topologically @IsOpenMap)
    (SpecIso n R).inv, SpecIso_inv_over]
  exact MvPolynomial.isOpenMap_comap_C

instance : GeometricallyIrreducible (𝔸(n; S) ↘ S) := by
  rw [geometricallyIrreducible_iff]
  introv K h
  apply ObjectProperty.prop_of_iso _
    ((h.isoIsPullback _ _ (isPullback_map _)) ≪≫ (SpecIso n (.of K))).symm
  infer_instance

instance [IrreducibleSpace S] : IrreducibleSpace 𝔸(n; S) :=
  GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace (𝔸(n; S) ↘ S) (isOpenMap_over S)

instance : GeometricallyReduced (𝔸(n; S) ↘ S) := by
  rw [geometricallyReduced_iff]
  introv K h
  apply ObjectProperty.prop_of_iso _
    ((h.isoIsPullback _ _ (isPullback_map _)) ≪≫ (SpecIso n (.of K))).symm
  infer_instance

instance [h : IsReduced S] : IsReduced 𝔸(n; S) := by
  wlog hS : ∃ R, S = Spec R
  · rw [IsReduced.iff_of_openCover _ (S.affineCover.pullback₁ (𝔸(n; S) ↘ S))]
    intro i
    have : IsReduced 𝔸(n; S.affineCover.X i) := this _ ⟨_, rfl⟩
    exact isReduced_of_isOpenImmersion ((isPullback_map _).isoPullback.inv)
  obtain ⟨R, rfl⟩ := hS
  rw [affine_isReduced_iff] at h
  exact isReduced_of_isOpenImmersion (SpecIso n R).hom

instance : GeometricallyIntegral (𝔸(n; S) ↘ S) :=
  .of_geometricallyReduced_of_geometricallyIrreducible _

os.makedirs('/tmp/t14', exist_ok=True)
labels=['proj-scheme','affinespace-pattern','morphism-classes','mvpoly-graded','glue-engine','graded-ring-project','blueprint-nodes','proj-functor-basechange']
for line in open(path):
    j=json.loads(line)

        key=j['key']  # key includes prompt hash? try to match by index
        r=j['result']
        # match by which label appears in the summary/prompt-free heuristic: use declarations file paths
        s=json.dumps(r)
        best=None
        if 'ProjectiveSpectrum/Scheme.lean' in s and 'toLocallyRingedSpace' in s: best='proj-scheme'
        if 'AffineSpace' in s and 'homOverEquiv' in s.replace(' ',''): best=best or 'affinespace-pattern'
        if 'IsStableUnderBaseChange' in s and 'ClosedImmersion' in s and 'Proper.lean' in s: best=best or 'morphism-classes'
        if 'homogeneousSubmodule' in s and 'GradeZero' in s: best=best or 'mvpoly-graded'
        if 'GlueDescent' in s: best=best or 'glue-engine'
        if 'sectionDeg' in s: best=best or 'graded-ring-project'
        if 'Picard_QuotScheme.tex' in s and 'quot_boundedness' in s: best=best or 'blueprint-nodes'
        if 'GradedRingHom' in s and 'Functor.lean' in s: best=best or 'proj-functor-basechange'
        fn = best or key
        with open(f'/tmp/t14/{fn}.json','a') as f: json.dump(r,f,indent=1)
        print(fn, len(s))
"

122	 "summary": "The project's module-gluing engine is Archon-original (Mathlib has no module descent over a scheme glue datum) and lives in AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/GlueDescent.lean, namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules. (a) Entry point: `Scheme.Modules.glue` (GlueDescent.lean:106) takes a `D : Scheme.GlueData`, per-chart sheaves `M : \u2200 i, (D.U i).Modules`, transition isos `g : \u2200 i j, (pullback (D.f i j)).obj (M i) \u2245 (pullback (D.t i j \u226b D.f j i)).obj (M j)`, a C1 self-identity field (g i i = eqToIso cast) and a C2 triple-overlap field (composite of pullbackBaseChangeTransport's interleaved with pullbackCongr casts along glueData_bridge_src/mid/tgt), returning `D.glued.Modules` as an equalizer of pushforwards. Morphisms into it via `glueLift` (:176) with overlap hypothesis checkable in pullback form via `glueLift_cond_iff` (:770). (b) The cover is a Mathlib `Scheme.GlueData` \u2014 NOT an OpenCover or opens directly; opens covers are converted via `opensCover \u2026 |>.gluedCover` (covGD, GrassmannianZariskiSheaf.lean:495) and the result transported back along the iso `fromGlued`. Overlaps are the abstract V(i,j) with immersions D.f i j and flips D.t i j. (c) Transition data: per-pair isos in X.Modules between the two pullbacks of the chart sheaves to V(i,j), target twisted through t i j. (d) Compatibility: C1 (diagonal transition = canonical eqToIso cast) and C2 (transported cocycle \u011d_jk \u2218 \u011d_ij = \u011d_ik on V_ijk), in the exact shapes above; they are consumed only by effective descent, not by the construction. (e) Worked line/free-bundle example: `universalQuotient` (GrassmannianQuot.lean:1835) glues free rank-d sheaves `SheafOfModules.free (Fin d)` over affine charts with transitions `bundleTransition` (:418) = pullbackFreeIso \u226a\u226b matrixToFreeIso (X^I_J)\u207b\u00b9 \u226a\u226b pullbackFreeIso.symm \u2014 for O(m) on Proj imitate with d=1 and the 1\u00d71 matrix (t_i/t_j)^m in \u0393(D\u208a(t_i t_j),O); C1/C2 reduce to matrix identities via matrixToFreeIso_mul and generic cast-collapse lemmas. (f) Recovery: `glueRestrictionIso D M g hC1 hC2 i : (pullback (D.\u03b9 i)).obj (glue \u2026) \u2245 M i` (:3371, via isIso_glueRestrictionHom :3330, universe 0 only); lifted morphisms recover via pullback_map_glueLift_glueRestrictionHom (:1075) / glueLift_glueProj (:1012). Basics: `X.Modules := SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf`, `Scheme.Modules.pullback/pushforward/pullbackPushforwardAdjunction/pullbackComp/pullbackCongr/pushforwardCongr` all from Mathlib (Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Modules/Sheaf.lean:37,164,180,185,232,248,237); unit = `SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` (project alias `unitModule`, SectionGradedRing.lean:108); tensor = project-local `Modules.sheafTensorObj`/`tensorPow`/`moduleTensorPow` (SectionGradedRing.lean:99/115) with pullback comparisons in QuotFunctorDef.lean."

2	 "summary": "The section graded ring/module lives in AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/SectionGradedRing.lean (4251 lines) as UNBUNDLED graded-piece families, not a named 'SectionGradedRing' type. The graded ring \u2295_m \u0393(X, L^\u2297m) is the family `sectionDeg L : \u2115 \u2192 Type u` (degree-m carrier = global sections of `tensorPow L m`) equipped with `GradedMonoid.GMul`/`GOne` instances, then non-instance defs `sectionGradedRing_gmonoid` \u2192 `sectionGradedRing_gsemiring : DirectSum.GSemiring (sectionDeg L)` (any L) \u2192 `sectionGradedRing_gcommSemiring : DirectSum.GCommSemiring (sectionDeg L)` (needs `[IsInvertibleGr L]`). The actual ring on the direct sum is only materialized as `Nonempty (Semiring (DirectSum \u2115 (sectionDeg L)))` / `Nonempty (CommSemiring ...)` sanity theorems (instances are deliberately not registered because carriers depend on L; brought into scope with letI). The graded module \u2295_m \u0393(X, F \u2297 L^\u2297m) is `moduleSectionDeg F L : \u2115 \u2192 Type u` (sections of `moduleTensorPow F L m = sheafTensorObj F (tensorPow L m)`) with `sectionGradedModule_gmodule : DirectSum.Gmodule (sectionDeg L) (moduleSectionDeg F L)` (no invertibility needed; action comparison iso is `moduleTensorPowAdd`). \u03ba(s)-structures on fibre sections come from `Scheme.Hom.fiberSectionsModule` = `Module.compHom \u0393(G, \u22a4) (\u03c0.fiberResidueMap s).hom`, where `fiberResidueMap : S.residueField s \u27f6 \u0393(\u03c0.fiber s, \u22a4)` is `(\u0393SpecIso (S.residueField s)).inv \u226b (\u03c0.fiberToSpecResidueField s).appTop`. HilbertPolynomial.lean defines `Scheme.hilbertFunction \u03c0 L F s m = Module.finrank \u03ba(s) \u0393(X_s, F_s \u2297 L_s^\u2297m)` (junk 0 if infinite) and `Scheme.hilbertPolynomial` as classical choice of the unique \u211a-polynomial eventually matching it (junk 0 if none); the bridge is `existsUnique_hilbertPolynomial_of_isRatHilb` consuming `IsRatHilb (fun m => (hilbertFunction \u03c0 L F s m : \u211a)) d`. The engine `gradedModule_hilbertSeries_rational` (GradedHilbertSerre.lean:1243) does NOT take the graded ring at all: it wants a \u03ba-vector space M with a decomposition \u2133 : \u2115 \u2192 Submodule \u03ba M ([DirectSum.Decomposition \u2133], [\u2200 n, FiniteDimensional \u03ba (\u2133 n)]) plus r pairwise-commuting degree-raising endomorphisms t : Fin r \u2192 Module.End \u03ba M such that M is Module.Finite over MvPolynomial (Fin r) \u03ba via `polyModule t hcomm = Module.compHom M (polyEndHom t hcomm)`. So a consumable `sectionGradedModule_fg` should be stated in exactly that MvPolynomial/endomorphism shape (not as Module.Finite over `DirectSum \u2115 (sectionDeg L)` as a plain ring, and not Algebra.FiniteType): for proper X_s/ample L_s/coherent F_s, produce (a) FiniteDimensional \u03ba(s) on each \u0393(X_s, F_s \u2297 L_s^\u2297m) under fiberSectionsModule (this is itself Serre finiteness of H\u2070 and is ALSO needed separately so finrank isn't junk), and (b) an r, degree-one multiplication endomorphisms t\u1d62 (graded action by chosen x_i \u2208 \u0393(X_s, L_s), commuting via the GCommSemiring layer / symmetric braiding, degree-raising by construction) on M = DirectSum \u2115 (moduleSectionDeg F_s L_s) given componentwise \u03ba(s)-structure, with Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin r) \u03ba(s)) M. If one prefers to state fg over the graded ring (Module.Finite over DirectSum \u2115 (sectionDeg L) with letI := sectionGradedRing_gsemiring), a second transfer lemma to the MvPolynomial form would be required \u2014 the engine gives no adapter. Ample/very-ample vocabulary exists ONLY in docstrings/comments (HilbertPolynomial.lean:44,141,219; QuotScheme.lean:64 'very ample L', :154 'Serre vanishing for proper X_s and ample L_s'; CechTermAcyclic.lean:685 relative Serre vanishing remark); no formal Lean definition or use of IsAmple/very-ampleness anywhere in the project.",

A consumable sectionGradedModule_fg must therefore either (a) be stated directly in the engine's shape \u2014 exhibit M := DirectSum \u2115 (moduleSectionDeg F_s L_s) as a \u03ba(s)-module (componentwise via fiberSectionsModule), grading submodules \u2133 n := range of the degree-n inclusion with Decomposition, \u2200 n FiniteDimensional, plus r commuting degree-raising endomorphisms (multiplication by chosen degree-1 sections x_i \u2208 \u0393(X_s, L_s)) with Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin r) \u03ba(s)) M \u2014 or (b) be stated as classical fg over DirectSum \u2115 (sectionDeg L) (letI := sectionGradedRing_gsemiring) and pay for an extra transfer lemma; no such adapter exists in the project.",

The endomorphisms t i must be \u03ba(s)-linear, which requires the graded action of \u0393(X_s, L_s) to commute with the \u03ba(s)-scalars coming through \u0393(X_s, \ud835\udcaa) \u2014 this compatibility is not yet stated anywhere.",
